name: DeepSeek-V4-Flash (Think Max)
archDisplay: Mixture-of-Experts (284B total / 13B active), hybrid Compressed Sparse Attention + Heavily Compressed Attention
note: Smaller, cheaper sibling MoE released alongside V4-Pro on the same day; fast default mode. (maximum reasoning-effort tier / 'Pro-Max' mode, DeepSeek's most capable released configuration).

results.notes: Terminal-Bench 2.0, Think Max / Pro-Max mode. Cross-verified identical value in DeepSeek-V4 tech report Table 7 (arXiv:2606.19348).
BrowseComp Pass@1, Think Max / Pro-Max mode. Cross-verified identical value in DeepSeek-V4 tech report Table 7 (arXiv:2606.19348).
GDPval-AA Elo, Think Max / Pro-Max mode. Only reported for the Max reasoning tier (dash for Non-Think/High). Cross-verified identical value in DeepSeek-V4 tech report Table 7 (arXiv:2606.19348).

SealedBench sealed capability index — composite of capability, token efficiency, speed and cost; rank 20/28 on the 2026-07-22 run (sealedbench.com). Listed as "DeepSeek V4 Flash" (DeepSeek). Evaluated at the highest available thinking setting. Attached to the Think Max effort sibling of DeepSeek-V4-Flash (highest available thinking).

verificationNotes:
Confirmed via official DeepSeek api-docs news post and the deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Flash HF model card (284B total/13B active, MIT, 1M context, same three-tier benchmark table format as V4-Pro).

2026-07-16: Checked mmlu, math, aime, humaneval, mmmu for DeepSeek-V4-Flash (Think Max). Official DeepSeek sources (HF model card's 'Instruct Model' tables spanning Non-Think/Think High/Think Max, and the macaron.im transcription of the same figures) report MMLU (88.7), MATH (57.4), and HumanEval (69.5) only under a distinct 'Base Model' section for the separate DeepSeek-V4-Flash-Base pretrained checkpoint -- none of the three appear in any instruct/reasoning-tuned tier's table. Independent third-party trackers corroborate the absence: benchlm.ai lists 40 tracked benchmarks for Flash (Max) and none of the three appear; artificialanalysis.ai's Intelligence Index breakdown for the Flash reasoning-tier page lists its own newer benchmark suite (GDPval-AA v2, Terminal-Bench v2.1, SciCode, HLE, GPQA Diamond, AA-Omniscience, IFBench, MMMU-Pro, etc.) with no MMLU/MATH/HumanEval row either. AIME is absent everywhere checked for the Flash line -- DeepSeek's own V4 competition-math benchmark of choice is HMMT 2026 Feb, not AIME; one low-credibility aggregator (layerlens.ai, citing an unverifiable 'Stratix evaluation' hash with no reasoning-tier specified) claims 96.7%, but was rejected as not meeting the sourcing bar -- it has zero corroboration from HF, the arXiv tech report, macaron.im, benchlm.ai, or artificialanalysis.ai, and a separate 99.4% AIME 'leak' for this same model was independently confirmed fake (per a widely-cited debunking), indicating this specific figure attracts fabricated claims. MMMU is not applicable: this model is text-only (capabilities.vision=false, modalities=[\"text\"]), and MMMU is a multimodal/vision benchmark; no MMMU score appears in any source checked. All five sibling DeepSeek-V4 family corpus entries (Pro/Flash x Non-Think/High/Max) independently show this identical five-benchmark gap, supporting a genuine reporting absence rather than a research shortfall.

2026-07-17 (round-2 deep pass, mmlu/math/aime/humaneval): Checked all 6 newly-specified source types beyond the first pass's official-launch/Epoch/Vals/LMArena/BenchLM sweep. (1) Stanford HELM: the Lite and Capabilities leaderboard pages (crfm.stanford.edu/helm/lite/latest, /helm/capabilities/latest) are JS-rendered SPAs that returned no fetchable model/score data via WebFetch or WebSearch, and no search result placed DeepSeek-V4-Flash on any HELM run/leaderboard -- no evidence HELM has evaluated this model at all. (2) arXiv/academic: re-examined the DeepSeek-V4 tech report (arXiv:2606.19348, already used in-corpus for terminal-bench-2/browsecomp/gdpval-aa cross-verification) specifically for MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval rows for the Flash instruct/reasoning tiers -- confirmed via both direct fetch and multiple targeted WebSearch queries that the paper's comparison tables report MMLU-Pro/GPQA-Diamond for Flash but do not contain plain MMLU, MATH, AIME, or HumanEval scores for any Flash reasoning tier; only qualitative language ('Flash-Max exhibits lower performance in knowledge evaluations due to smaller parameter scale'). No other arXiv or Semantic-Scholar-indexed paper surfaced using DeepSeek-V4-Flash as a cited baseline with these four scores. (3) GitHub eval-harness logs: found two community quantization repos with partial numbers -- canada-quant/dsv4-flash-w4a16-fp8 reports HumanEval pass@1 80.49% and AIME-2024 '4/5 correct' for a W4A16-FP8 quantized build in think-max mode; andreaborio/forgequant reports no numeric scores at all. Rejected both as corpus additions: the AIME figure is an n=5 smoke-test sample (not a real AIME score), and both are run against quantized weights (not the released full-precision model this corpus entry represents), with no linked raw results.json or harness config to verify methodology. (4) Other leaderboards/aggregators: OpenRouter's model page (openrouter.ai/deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash) has a benchmarks section but displays no actual scores; ominigate.ai's benchmarks page explicitly lists only GPQA Diamond/HLE/SciCode/Terminal-Bench-Hard for Flash (citing Artificial Analysis, updated 2026-04-24) with no MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval row, corroborating the absence; two SEO/content-mill blogs (aimadetools.com, notelm.ai) do display MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval-shaped numbers for Flash, but both fail the sourcing bar -- aimadetools.com cites only vague 'DeepSeek's published evaluations and community reproductions' with no verifiable primary link, and notelm.ai explicitly does not distinguish 'DeepSeek V4' from 'DeepSeek V4 Flash' and was 'last verified January 10, 2026', i.e. before the model's April 24 2026 release, indicating templated/unreliable content. Given this corpus's documented precedent of fabricated AIME claims for this exact model (layerlens.ai 96.7%, a debunked 99.4% 'leak'), these uncorroborated blog numbers were not accepted. (5) Non-English sources: Chinese-language search (datalearner.com, CSDN, Zhihu) independently returned MMLU 88.7 / MATH 57.4 / HumanEval 69.5 -- but these corroborate the existing verificationNotes finding that these three numbers belong to the separate DeepSeek-V4-Flash-Base pretrained checkpoint, not any instruct/reasoning tier; direct fetch of datalearner.com and the Zhihu thread failed (DNS/403) so could not extract a tier-disaggregated table. (6) Sibling models: DeepSeek-V4-Pro's own launch materials and the shared tech report were checked for a comparison table carrying Flash's MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval as a reference point -- none found; Pro's official table likewise only carries MMLU-Pro/GPQA-Diamond, not plain MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval, for either sibling. Conclusion: genuine reporting absence confirmed by a second, methodologically distinct pass across all 6 required new source types -- no credible primary citation exists for mmlu, math, aime, or humaneval on this specific model/reasoning-tier. No additions made.

2026-07-18 (round-22, mmmu gap re-check): Re-confirmed MMMU is architecturally inapplicable to this corpus entry. Corpus fields are unambiguous: modalities=["text"], capabilities.vision=false. Rather than stopping at the corpus field alone, ran an additional external-corroboration pass per the task's research method: (1) WebSearch for "DeepSeek-V4-Flash MMMU vision benchmark" surfaced only unrelated DeepSeek-VL / DeepSeek-VL2 papers (arXiv:2403.05525, arXiv:2412.10302) -- a wholly separate, older DeepSeek model family for vision-language work, not V4/V4-Flash -- plus generic benchmark-aggregator pages (llm-stats.com/models/deepseek-v4-flash-max, benchlm.ai/models/deepseek-v4-flash, openrouter.ai/deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash) that list only text benchmarks (GPQA, IFBench, Agentic Index, Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index, etc.) with no MMMU row. (2) A second targeted WebSearch explicitly returned third-party confirmation: "Both V4 Flash and V4 Pro are text-only as of April 2026. DeepSeek has said multimodal capabilities are in development, but no timeline has been published" (per buildfastwithai.com/blogs/deepseek-v4-flash-review-2026 and corroborating aggregator pages), matching the corpus's own capabilities.vision=false. No DeepSeek-V4-Flash-VL or multimodal variant exists as of this check. Conclusion: MMMU is architecturally inapplicable (text-only model, no vision capability) -- not a reporting gap. No score exists or can exist for this model/benchmark pair. No addition made; proposing omitted_no_evidence per the task's explicit step-6 instruction.
